title: Mercimek Corbasi Red Lentil Soup

seo.imageAlt: Bowl of authentic Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) garnished with fresh mint.
seo.imageCaption: Warm and comforting, this homemade Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi) is a staple of Turkish cuisine.

description: Mercimek Corbasi is a beloved Turkish red lentil soup, known for its vibrant color and comforting warmth. This hearty soup is incredibly simple to prepare, combining earthy red lentils with aromatic vegetables and a signature spiced butter drizzle. It's a staple in Turkish homes, perfect for a nourishing meal any time of year.

instructions.title: Prepare Ingredients
instructions.text: Rinse the red lentils thoroughly under cold running water until the water runs clear; drain well. Finely chop the onion, carrot, and potato (if using) into small, even pieces for quick cooking.

instructions.title: Saute Aromatics
instructions.text: In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat the olive oil over medium heat. Add the chopped onion, carrot, and potato. Saute for 5-7 minutes until the vegetables soften and become fragrant, stirring occasionally.

instructions.title: Add Tomato Paste and Seasonings
instructions.text: Stir in the tomato paste and cook for another minute, stirring constantly to toast the paste and deepen its flavor. Add the rinsed lentils, salt, and black pepper to the pot, mixing well to combine with the vegetables.

instructions.title: Simmer the Soup Base
instructions.text: Pour in the water or vegetable broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low. Cover the pot and simmer for 25-30 minutes, or until the lentils and all vegetables are very tender. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

instructions.title: Blend for Smoothness
instructions.text: Remove the pot from the heat. Using an immersion blender, blend the soup directly in the pot until it is completely smooth and creamy. Alternatively, carefully transfer the soup in batches to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return to the pot. Adjust consistency with more hot water if desired.

instructions.title: Prepare Spiced Butter Topping
instructions.text: In a small pan, melt the butter over medium-low heat. Once the butter is melted and slightly foamy, remove from heat and stir in the dried mint and red pepper flakes. The residual heat from the butter will bloom the spices, releasing their full aroma and color.

instructions.title: Serve Mercimek Corbasi
instructions.text: Ladle the hot Mercimek Corbasi into individual serving bowls. Drizzle a generous amount of the fragrant spiced butter topping over each serving. Serve immediately with fresh lemon wedges on the side for squeezing, as a squeeze of lemon brightens the flavors beautifully.

tips: For the smoothest soup, ensure all vegetables and lentils are very tender before blending. An immersion blender makes this step quick and easy, minimizing clean-up.
The spiced butter (nane yagli sos) is crucial for authentic flavor. Don't skip it! Adjust the amount of red pepper flakes to your spice preference for a mild or spicier kick.
If the soup is too thick after blending, thin it with a little hot water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency. Always taste and adjust seasoning as needed before serving.
article:
Discover the heartwarming embrace of Turkish cuisine with Mercimek Corbasi, the beloved Turkish Red Lentil Soup. This vibrant, nutritious, and incredibly flavorful soup is a staple in Turkish homes, gracing tables from bustling city restaurants to cozy family dinners. Known for its creamy texture, rich lentil flavor, and a signature drizzle of chili-infused mint butter, Mercimek Corbasi is more than just a soup; it's a comforting hug in a bowl, a testament to the simplicity and depth of Turkish culinary traditions. Whether you're seeking a healthy, satisfying meal or an authentic taste of Turkey, this guide will walk you through everything you need to know about preparing this iconic dish.

## What is Turkish Red Lentil Soup (Mercimek Corbasi)?

Mercimek Corbasi, pronounced "mer-jee-MEK chor-BAH-see," translates directly to "lentil soup" and specifically refers to the bright orange-red lentil variety. It's a cornerstone of Turkish soups and stews, cherished for its ease of preparation, affordability, and incredible nutritional value. Unlike some heartier stews, this soup is typically light, smooth, and incredibly soothing, making it a popular choice for breakfast, lunch, or a starter before a larger meal.

Originating from the Anatolian region, Mercimek Corbasi embodies the essence of Turkish home cooking: simple, fresh ingredients transformed into something extraordinary. The soup’s distinctive color comes from red lentils, which cook down quickly into a naturally creamy consistency. Its flavor profile is a delicate balance of earthy lentils, sweet sautéed onions, a touch of tangy tomato paste, and often, a vibrant kick from dried mint and red pepper flakes sizzled in butter. It's a dish that warms the soul and satisfies the palate without being heavy, reflecting the healthy and balanced nature of the Mediterranean diet. The deep comfort derived from a bowl of this Turkish Red Lentil Soup is truly unmatched.

## Ingredients for Mercimek Corbasi

The beauty of Mercimek Corbasi lies in its humble yet powerful ingredients. Each component plays a crucial role in building the soup's characteristic flavor and texture.

For the Soup Base:
*   **Red Lentils (Kırmızı Mercimek):** The star of the show. Red lentils cook quickly and break down easily, giving the soup its signature creamy texture and earthy flavor.
*   **Onion:** Provides a foundational sweetness and aromatic depth when sautéed.
*   **Garlic:** Adds a pungent, savory layer of flavor that complements the lentils beautifully.
*   **Tomato Paste (Salça):** A small amount adds color, umami, and a subtle tanginess, deepening the overall flavor.
*   **Water or Vegetable Broth:** The liquid base. Broth can add an extra layer of savory flavor.
*   **Salt & Black Pepper:** Essential seasonings to bring out all the flavors.
*   **Optional (for thickening):** A small amount of flour can be used, though many traditional recipes rely solely on the lentils for thickness.

For the Nane Yağı (Mint Butter Sauce) - Crucial for Authenticity:
*   **Unsalted Butter:** The base for the fragrant drizzle. Olive oil can be substituted for a vegan version.
*   **Dried Mint (Kuru Nane):** The defining herb for Mercimek Corbasi, offering a fresh, aromatic counterpoint.
*   **Red Pepper Flakes (Pul Biber):** Adds a mild warmth and a beautiful red hue. Adjust to your spice preference. Smoked paprika can also be a nice addition.

These simple ingredients, when combined with care, create the rich and comforting Turkish Red Lentil Soup that has delighted generations.

## Step-by-Step: How to Make Turkish Red Lentil Soup

Making authentic Mercimek Corbasi is surprisingly straightforward, even for novice cooks. Follow these steps for a perfect bowl every time:

1.  **Prepare the Lentils:** Start by thoroughly rinsing 1 cup of red lentils under cold running water until the water runs clear. This removes any debris and excess starch, which can make the soup cloudy. Drain well.

2.  **Sauté the Aromatics:** In a large pot or Dutch oven, heat 1-2 tablespoons of olive oil or butter over medium heat. Add 1 finely chopped medium onion and sauté until softened and translucent, about 5-7 minutes. Add 2-3 cloves of minced garlic and cook for another minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.

3.  **Add Tomato Paste and Lentils:** Stir in 1 tablespoon of tomato paste and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, to deepen its flavor. Add the rinsed and drained red lentils to the pot and stir to coat them with the onion and tomato paste mixture.

4.  **Add Liquid and Simmer:** Pour in 6-7 cups of water or vegetable broth. Bring the mixture to a boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 20-25 minutes, or until the lentils are very soft and starting to break down. Stir occasionally to prevent sticking.

5.  **Blend for Creaminess:** Once the lentils are tender, remove the pot from the heat. Use an immersion blender to blend the soup directly in the pot until it's smooth and creamy. If you don't have an immersion blender, carefully transfer batches of the soup to a regular blender and blend until smooth, then return it to the pot. Be cautious when blending hot liquids.

6.  **Season and Adjust:** Return the pot to low heat. Season the soup with salt and black pepper to taste. If the soup is too thick, add a little more hot water or broth until it reaches your desired consistency. Let it gently simmer for another 5 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.

7.  **Prepare the Mint Butter Sauce (Nane Yağı):** While the soup is simmering, melt 2 tablespoons of unsalted butter in a small pan over medium-low heat. Once the butter is melted and slightly foamy, add 1-2 teaspoons of dried mint and 1/2 - 1 teaspoon of red pepper flakes (or pul biber). Sauté for about 30 seconds until fragrant, being careful not to burn the mint or spices.

8.  **Serve:** Ladle the hot Turkish Red Lentil Soup into bowls. Drizzle a spoonful of the fragrant mint butter sauce over each serving. Garnish with fresh lemon wedges and serve immediately with crusty bread.

## Pro Tips for the Best Mercimek Corbasi

To elevate your Turkish Red Lentil Soup from good to absolutely unforgettable, keep these expert tips in mind:

*   **Rinse Those Lentils!** This step is crucial. Rinsing red lentils thoroughly removes starches that can make your soup gummy or cloudy.
*   **Don't Skip the Tomato Paste Sauté:** Cooking the tomato paste for a minute or two before adding liquids deepens its flavor and removes any raw taste, contributing significantly to the soup's richness.
*   **The Power of the Mint Butter:** The *nane yağı* (mint butter sauce) is non-negotiable for an authentic Mercimek Corbasi. It adds a burst of aromatic freshness and a touch of warmth from the chili flakes that truly defines the soup's character.
*   **Fresh Lemon is Key:** A squeeze of fresh lemon juice just before serving brightens the entire dish, cutting through the richness and adding a delightful tang that balances the flavors perfectly. Don't underestimate its impact!
*   **Adjust Consistency to Your Liking:** Some prefer a thicker soup, others a thinner one. Feel free to add more hot water or broth after blending if you desire a looser consistency.
*   **Quality Ingredients Matter:** While simple, using good quality red lentils, fresh onions and garlic, and a flavorful tomato paste will make a noticeable difference in the final taste.
*   **Season as You Go (and at the End):** Taste your soup before and after blending. Lentils absorb a lot of salt, so you might need to adjust the seasoning at the end.
*   **Let it Rest (Optional):** Like many soups, the flavors of Mercimek Corbasi can deepen and meld even further if allowed to rest for 10-15 minutes after blending before serving.

## Serving Suggestions & Variations

Mercimek Corbasi is wonderfully versatile, perfect on its own or as part of a larger Turkish meal.

**Traditional Serving:**
*   **Lemon Wedges:** Absolutely essential! A generous squeeze of fresh lemon juice over each bowl brightens the flavors.
*   **Crusty Bread:** Turkish pide bread or any crusty bread is perfect for dipping and soaking up every last drop.
*   **Pickles:** A small side of Turkish pickles (turşu) offers a delightful tangy contrast.
*   **Extra Drizzle:** Don't be shy with an extra swirl of the mint butter sauce!

**Variations to Explore:**
*   **Vegetable Boost:** For added nutrition and depth, sauté finely diced carrots and potatoes along with the onion at the beginning. They will cook down with the lentils and blend seamlessly.
*   **Spicier Kick:** Increase the amount of red pepper flakes in the mint butter sauce, or add a pinch of cayenne pepper directly to the soup base.
*   **Smoky Flavor:** A tiny pinch of smoked paprika in the mint butter sauce can add a lovely smoky undertone.
*   **Herbal Additions:** While dried mint is traditional, a sprinkle of fresh chopped parsley or cilantro can be a nice garnish for those who enjoy it.
*   **Vegan Option:** Easily make this Turkish Red Lentil Soup vegan by substituting olive oil for butter in both the soup base and the mint butter sauce.
*   **Stock Swap:** While water is traditional, using vegetable broth (or even chicken broth if not strictly vegetarian) can add more savory depth.

## Frequently Asked Questions

**Q: Is Turkish Red Lentil Soup spicy?**
A: Typically, Mercimek Corbasi is not inherently spicy. The heat comes from the red pepper flakes (pul biber) in the mint butter sauce, which is usually added to taste. You can adjust the amount of pepper flakes or omit them entirely if you prefer a milder soup.

**Q: Can I make Mercimek Corbasi vegan?**
A: Absolutely! This soup is very easy to make vegan. Simply substitute olive oil for butter when sautéing the aromatics and in the mint butter sauce. Ensure you use vegetable broth instead of chicken broth, or just water.

**Q: What kind of lentils should I use?**
A: For authentic Mercimek Corbasi, you must use red lentils (kırmızı mercimek). They cook quickly and break down easily, creating the signature creamy texture. Other types of lentils (green, brown) will not yield the same result.

**Q: Can I freeze Mercimek Corbasi?**
A: Yes, Mercimek Corbasi freezes very well. Allow the soup to cool completely, then transfer it to airtight containers or freezer-safe bags. It can be stored in the freezer for up to 3 months. Thaw overnight in the refrigerator and reheat gently on the stovetop, adding a little water or broth if needed to adjust consistency.

**Q: How long does Mercimek Corbasi last in the refrigerator?**
A: Leftover Mercimek Corbasi can be stored in an airtight container in the refrigerator for 3-4 days. It often tastes even better the next day as the flavors have more time to meld.

**Q: Do I need to soak the red lentils before cooking?**
A: No, red lentils do not require soaking. They cook very quickly, usually within 20-25 minutes, directly from their dry state after a good rinse.

Embrace the warmth and flavor of Turkish cuisine by making this incredible Turkish Red Lentil Soup. It's a comforting, healthy, and deeply satisfying dish that will transport your taste buds straight to the heart of Turkey. Enjoy!
